case_title: SANDRA LEON VS. KRISTIN KOJAN et al

docket_entries.description: CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEON, SANDRA JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 10.0 DAYS

DISCOVERY, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NO. 27 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION THE REQUEST IS NOT STATED WITH REASONABLE PARTICULARITY IS SUSTAINED IN THE CONTEXT OF PLAINTIFF'S PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS RESPONDING TO MULTIPLE OTHER HIGHLY SPECIFIC DOCUMENT CATEGORIES. SEE CCP 2031.030 (C) (1) AND CALCOR SPACE FACILITY, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1997) 53 CAL. APP. 4TH 216. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING HER CONTENTION THAT DEFENDANT RECKLESSLY INSTRUCTED HER TO PERFORM AN EXERCISE THAT WENT BEYOND THE RANGE OF ORDINARY ACTIVITIES INVOLVED IN PROVIDING PERSONAL TRAINING SERVICES TO A PERSON OF PLAINTIFF'S AGE, WEIGHT, CONDITIONING, AND EXPERIENCE. DISCOVERY, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 12.2 IS DENIED. THE SUPREME COURT IN COITO V. SUPERIOR COURT, (2012) 54 CAL. 4TH 480 SOLELY REFERRED TO FORM INTEROGATORY 17.3, LEAVING INTACT THE DECISION IN NACHT & LEWIS ARCHITECTS, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 47 CAL. APP. 4TH 214. THE NACHT DECISION HELD THAT "COMPELLED PRODUCTION OF A LIST OF POTENTIAL WITNESSES INTERVIEWED BY OPPOSING COUNSEL WOULD NECESSARILY REFLECT COUNSEL'S EVALUATION OF THE CASE BY REVEALING WHICH WITNESSES OR PERSONS WHO CLAIMED KNOWLEDGE OF THE INCIDENT (ALREADY IDENTIFIED BY DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORY NO. 12.1) COUNSEL DEEMED IMPORTANT ENOUGH TO INTERVIEW." DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts supporting her contention that Defendant recklessly instructed her to perform an exercise that went beyond the range of ordinary activities involved in providing personal training services to a person of plaintiff's age, weight, conditioning, and experience. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/eg

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltff Sandra Leon'S Further Responses To Deft'S Request For Production Of Documents No. 27
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Sumner, Esq., 925.278.6170, for plaintiff Sandra Leon.
Yaniv Newman, Esq., 415.278.1418, for defendant Kristen Kojan.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN's
Motion to compel further response to Request for Production of Documents No. 27 is denied. Plaintiff's objection the request is not stated with reasonable particularity is sustained in the context of plaintiff's production of documents responding to multiple other highly specific document categories. See CCP 2031.030 (c) (1) and Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Superior Court (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 216. Defendant to submit proposed order. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 12.2
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Sumner, Esq., 925.278.6170, for plaintiff Sandra Leon.
Yaniv Newman, Esq., 415.278.1418, for defendant Kristen Kojan.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN's Motion to compel a further response to Form Interrogatory 12.2 is denied. The Supreme Court in Coito v. Superior Court, (2012) 54 Cal. 4th 480 solely referred to Form Interogatory 17.3, leaving intact the decision in Nacht & Lewis Architects, Inc. v. Superior Court (1996) 47 Cal. App. 4th 214. The Nacht decision held that "compelled production of a list of potential witnesses interviewed by opposing counsel would necessarily reflect counsel's evaluation of the case by revealing which witnesses or persons who claimed knowledge of the incident (already identified by defendants' response to interrogatory No. 12.1) counsel deemed important enough to interview." Defendant to prepare proposed order. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.
